title: Coleostephus paludosus
intro: Coleostephus myriocephalus, also known as Coleostephus paludosus, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of Coleostephus paludosus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Herbal preparations made from Coleostephus paludosus include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Coleostephus paludosus belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its composite flowers and seed head. The genus Coleostephus is part of the tribe Anthemideae, which includes various daisy-like plants. Further classification places Coleostephus paludosus in the subfamily Asteroideae. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other members of the Asteraceae family.
